message:
feat(nn,optim,conformance): add activation modules, loss functions, LR schedulers, and oracle override support

Add 8 new nn activation modules (SELU, Hardtanh, Softshrink, Hardshrink,
Tanhshrink, Softsign, Softmin) and Reduction enum for loss functions.
Add 3 new loss modules (FocalLoss, MultiMarginLoss, SoftMarginLoss) with
full reduction support and comprehensive tests.

Add PolynomialLR and ConstantLR learning rate schedulers to ft-optim with
state serialization roundtrip tests.

Make the legacy PyTorch oracle root configurable via FT_LEGACY_ORACLE_ROOT
env var, with fallback to the conventional local mirror path. Update
conformance harness default_paths() to use the new override mechanism
and add unit tests for both override and fallback paths.

Update spec and documentation to reflect current workspace crate families,
revised architecture flow, 2321 passing tests, and higher-layer crate
inventory (ft-nn, ft-optim, ft-data).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 Modes tested for all listed families: strict + hardened.
-2240 tests passing across workspace.
+2321 tests passing across workspace.
 
 ## Gap Policy

@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@ Absolute doctrine carried from this project into all adaptations:
 | RaptorQ-everywhere artifact durability | RaptorQ sidecars for conformance fixtures, gradient mismatch bundles, and benchmark baselines |
 | strict vs hardened mode split | strict: PyTorch-observable parity; hardened: bounded defenses with fail-closed incompatibility handling |
 | evidence ledger for consequential runtime decisions | dispatch decision ledger + backward replay ledger for DAC proofs |
-| conformance harness as release gate | differential oracle harness against `legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch` with machine-readable parity reports |
+| conformance harness as release gate | differential oracle harness against a typically local `legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch` mirror, with machine-readable parity reports and explicit override support when the mirror lives elsewhere |
 | one-lever optimization doctrine + isomorphism proofs | each performance change must include gradient-isomorphism proof and benchmark delta |

 - Bind RaptorQ sidecar pipelines to asupersync transport/codec surfaces (mirroring FrankenSQLite usage).

+- `ft-optim`
+- `ft-data`

@@ -2,6 +2,9 @@
 
 This folder stores normalized oracle-vs-target fixtures for `ft-conformance`.
 
+The legacy PyTorch oracle is typically mirrored at `legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch` relative to the
+repo root. Harness runs may override that default with `FT_LEGACY_ORACLE_ROOT`.
+
 - `smoke_case.json`: minimal bootstrap fixture ensuring harness wiring works.
 - `scalar_autograd_cases.json`: deterministic scalar DAC fixture family (strict + hardened).
 - `tensor_binary_cases.json`: deterministic tensor binary DAC fixture family (strict + hardened) for add/sub/mul/div forward and gradient parity.

@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 {
   "suite": "smoke",
-  "oracle": "legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch",
+  "oracle": "legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch (default local mirror; override with FT_LEGACY_ORACLE_ROOT)",
   "notes": "Bootstrap fixture for first runnable conformance harness"
 }
